recent.statement: A magnitude 6.5 earthquake will occur in the Philippines
recent.evidence: No signal reports a magnitude 6.5 earthquake in the Philippines, and such an event would certainly have been captured during the closed window.

recent.statement: A Category 2 hurricane will make landfall in Mexico within the next week
recent.evidence: All tropical cyclone signals (BERTHA, FAUSTO, ELIDA) are green alerts at Tropical Storm strength with zero population affected by Category 1+ winds, and none shows a Category 2 hurricane landfall in Mexico.

recent.statement: A Category 2 tropical cyclone will form in the Pacific Ocean within the next week
recent.evidence: East Pacific cyclones FAUSTO and ELIDA are only Tropical Storms with green alerts, and no signal shows any Category 2 cyclone forming.

recent.statement: The price of CL=F will increase by at least 2% within the next 24 hours.
recent.evidence: The current WTI (CL=F) snapshot shows only +1.85%, below the required 2% increase threshold.

recent.statement: The price of oil will increase by at least $5 per barrel within the next week
recent.evidence: Brent rose only +3.32% and WTI +2.17% in the snapshots, with no signal showing an actual $5/bbl weekly price increase.

recent.statement: WTI Crude Oil price will increase by at least 2% within the next 24 hours
recent.evidence: The final [now] snapshot S10 shows WTI at 86.48 (+1.85%), and prices ranged only 86.34→86.75 over the window—well below a 2% increase from the forecast baseline.

recent.statement: WTI Crude Oil price will increase by more than 2% in the next 24 hours
recent.evidence: The current WTI snapshot shows only +1.85%, below the required 2% increase threshold.

recent.statement: WTI Crude Oil price will drop by at least 2% within the next 24h
recent.evidence: The latest WTI snapshot shows the price at 86.48 $/bbl (+1.85%), rising rather than dropping at least 2% within the window.

recent.statement: The price of oil will increase by at least $2 per barrel due to supply chain disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz
recent.evidence: S10 shows reduced Hormuz transits (a condition, not a disruption event), and no signal attributes the oil price rise to Strait of Hormuz supply-chain disruption, so the compound claim is not confirmed.

recent.statement: The price of oil will surge by at least $10 per barrel within the next week due to supply chain disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.
recent.evidence: Brent at 93.88 (+3.15%) and WTI at 86.75 (+2.17%) show only single-digit percentage moves, nowhere near the required $10/bbl surge, despite reduced Hormuz transits.

recent.statement: Tropical cyclone BAVI-26 makes landfall in China
recent.evidence: No signal mentions cyclone BAVI-26 or a landfall in China; only unrelated green-alert cyclones (BERTHA, FAUSTO, ELIDA) far from China appear.

recent.statement: The European Aviation Safety Agency (EASA) will extend its airspace restrictions for Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, and the UAE within the next week
recent.evidence: No captured signal concerns this forecast, and an EASA extension of airspace restrictions for these Gulf states would certainly have been captured had it occurred.

self_calibration.note: Published headline probability is RAW conviction (unchanged); this is the honest companion. Calibration fixes overconfidence, not discrimination — see /calibration.
